research_task.objective: Conduct comprehensive research on E2E test dashboards and reporting platforms to discover innovative features, UI patterns, and capabilities that can enhance our Playwright test dashboard beyond what Currents offers.

success_criteria.criterion: Identify at least 5 competing/alternative platforms to Currents
Document unique features not found in Currents
Provide actionable feature recommendations with implementation complexity
Include screenshots or UI descriptions where relevant
Assess technical feasibility for our Next.js + Neon stack

current_stack.technology: Cloudflare R2 for artifacts (videos, traces, screenshots)

context.existing_currents_research: We have already researched Currents and identified these features to implement: Flaky test detection (retry_count > 0 AND status = 'passed') Test signatures for historical tracking Volume metrics (rate × frequency) Test Explorer with 8 views Error pattern grouping Timeline visualization Period comparison (current vs previous) Quarantine workflow for flaky tests GitHub PR comments integration Slack notifications

phase.name: discovery
phase.instruction: Search for and analyze the following E2E test dashboard platforms: Allure Report / Allure TestOps ReportPortal.io TestRail (by Gurock) Tesults QA Wolf Dashboard LambdaTest Analytics BrowserStack Test Observability Sauce Labs Insights Datadog Test Visibility Grafana + test metrics GitHub Actions native test reporting CircleCI Test Insights Any other relevant platforms discovered

phase.name: feature_extraction
phase.instruction: For each platform, extract and document: Unique features not found in Currents UI/UX patterns that stand out Data visualization approaches Collaboration and team features AI/ML capabilities Integration ecosystem Pricing model insights (free tier features)

phase.name: deep_dive
phase.instruction:
Conduct deep research on these specific topics:
Search for: "AI test analysis dashboard", "ML test failure prediction", "automatic test categorization", "smart test prioritization" Goal: Identify how AI can enhance test dashboards
Search for: "visual regression dashboard", "screenshot comparison UI", "pixel diff visualization", "visual testing analytics" Goal: Understand visual testing dashboard patterns
Search for: "test observability platform 2025", "distributed test tracing", "test telemetry dashboard", "OpenTelemetry testing" Goal: Modern observability approaches for testing
Search for: "test dashboard UX best practices", "developer-friendly test reports", "test results IDE integration", "real-time test streaming" Goal: Best DX patterns for test dashboards
Search for: "CI cost analytics", "test parallelization efficiency", "test suite optimization dashboard", "reduce CI minutes" Goal: Cost and efficiency tracking features

phase.name: synthesis
phase.instruction: Synthesize findings into actionable recommendations: Categorize features by implementation complexity (low/medium/high) Prioritize based on value/effort ratio Map features to our existing tech stack capabilities Identify features that differentiate from Currents Note any features requiring new infrastructure

structure.section: Brief overview of key findings and top 5 recommended features

section.name: feature_catalog
section.category:
Features we should definitely implement
Feature name What it does Which platform(s) have it low | medium | high How to implement with our stack
Features that would set us apart from Currents

structure.section:
Best UI/UX patterns discovered across platforms
Pattern name How it works Platform that implements it well How we could use it

AI/ML features that could enhance our dashboard
Feature name What AI/ML capability What data we need High-level approach

section.name: technical_considerations
section.subsection: Features that work well with Next.js + Neon + R2

structure.section:
Suggested phased approach for implementing features
Feature 1 Feature 2
Feature 3
Feature 4
All URLs and references used in research

formatting_rules.rule: Include code snippets for database schema changes
Provide SQL queries for new metrics where applicable
Link to official documentation when available
Note pricing tiers when relevant (especially free features)

constraints.constraint: Focus on features applicable to Playwright/E2E testing dashboards. Exclude unit testing specific features unless broadly applicable.
Prioritize features implementable with: - Server-side rendering (Next.js RSC) - PostgreSQL queries (Neon) - Object storage (R2) - Real-time updates (consider options)
Emphasize features that would make our dashboard unique, not just a clone of existing solutions.
Focus on features with clear implementation paths. Avoid vaporware or features requiring massive infrastructure.

search_queries.query: "playwright test dashboard" OR "e2e test reporting platform" 2025
"test analytics dashboard features" site:dev.to OR site:medium.com
"AI powered test analysis" OR "ML test failure prediction" 2025
"test observability" Datadog OR Grafana OR OpenTelemetry
"Currents vs" OR "Allure vs" test reporting comparison 2025

reference_material.existing_research: We have documented Currents features in docs/currents-research.md including: - Hierarchical data model (Organization > Project > Run > Group > Spec > Test > Attempt) - 6 main metrics (executions, avg duration, passed duration, non-flaky duration, failure rate, flakiness rate) - Volume metrics concept (rate × frequency) - Test Explorer with 8 views - Flaky test detection algorithm - Error pattern grouping - GitHub and Slack integrations
reference_material.our_current_schema: Tables: test_executions, test_results, test_artifacts See: scripts/001_create_test_tables.sql
